jtcPhases.name: Commander's Objectives, Targeting Guidance, and Intent
jtcPhases.purpose: Establish what the commander wants the joint force to accomplish through targeting: objectives, desired effects, priorities, restrictions, and intent. Translates strategic and operational objectives into actionable targeting direction.
keyOutputs: Restricted target list (RTL) and no-strike list (NSL) inputs

jtcPhases.name: Target Development and Prioritization
jtcPhases.purpose: Develop targets through systematic examination of potential target systems, identifying high-value (HVT) and high-payoff targets (HPT), and produce a prioritized candidate target list. Conduct target system analysis (TSA), characterize targets (functional, physical, technical), vet, and validate. Build the joint integrated prioritized target list (JIPTL).

jtcPhases.name: Capabilities Analysis
jtcPhases.purpose: Match available capabilities (lethal and nonlethal) to validated targets and desired effects. Analyze feasibility, weaponeering, and force availability to identify viable solutions. Output drives Phase 4 commander's decision and force assignment.
keyOutputs: Recommended capability options to satisfy desired effects
